description_long: This is a futuristic, angular airplane with a sleek, streamlined fuselage and sharply swept wings that extend outward and slightly downward from the main body. The cockpit is positioned at the front and is enclosed with a tinted, faceted canopy, suggesting advanced avionics and pilot protection. The wings feature prominent yellow and red accent markings, and each wingtip is equipped with vertical stabilizer fins. The tail section is robust, with twin vertical stabilizers and a wide, flat rear profile, giving the aircraft a stable and aggressive appearance. The fuselage and wings are primarily gray with white and black accents, and there are visible insignias and markings on the wings and tail. The overall design suggests a high-performance, possibly military or sci-fi inspired aircraft, built for speed and maneuverability.
description: A sleek, futuristic airplane with swept wings, twin vertical stabilizers, and a forward, enclosed cockpit. The design features angular lines, gray and white coloring, and bold accent markings.
description_view_0: Front view shows the pointed nose, wide swept wings, and the angular cockpit canopy dominating the center.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the elongated nose, forward cockpit, and the vertical stabilizers at the rear, with visible wing and tail markings.
description_view_2: Rear view displays the twin vertical stabilizers, wide flat tail, and the full span of the swept wings.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirrors view 1, showing the cockpit, wing structure, and tail configuration from the other side.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with sharply swept wings and downward-angled winglets at the tips.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines integrated into the rear fuselage.
attribute_tail_design: Twin vertical stabilizers with a wide, flat H-tail configuration.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear, with two main gear units and a single nose gear (implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a single-seat canopy and angular, faceted windows.
